name: Unity Bank
description_short: Unity Bank is a community-focused bank offering checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, business banking, and investment services with local lending teams and school-centered community initiatives.
description_long:
Unity Bank operates as a full-service community bank with a strong emphasis on local presence and personalized service. The institution maintains physical branch locations across multiple communities, including Augusta, Polson, and Rush City, with a routing number (0918-083-63) indicating active deposit operations. The bank positions itself as a relationship-driven lender committed to understanding customer needs through direct interaction and transparency.

Unity Bank's core offerings include deposit products (checking and savings accounts), certificate of deposit (CD) accounts with competitive rates, mortgage lending for home purchases and refinancing, and business banking services. The bank also provides credit card products and remote deposit capabilities. Beyond traditional banking, Unity Bank offers comprehensive investment management, retirement planning, estate planning, and succession planning services through Unity Wealth Management (a marketing name for Cetera Investment Services). The bank actively promotes credit education initiatives and community engagement programs.

Unity Bank distinguishes itself through its School Spirit Debit Card program, which donates $0.05 per purchase to local school districts—generating $11,706.50 in contributions to nine local schools in the most recent reported period. The bank emphasizes personal mortgage lending with a "local lending team" approach and offers free financial literacy and credit education sessions at branch locations. Their online banking platform provides digital account access alongside traditional in-person service channels.

As a regional community bank, Unity Bank faces inherent limitations compared to national institutions: the geographic footprint appears limited to Minnesota (based on mentioned branch locations), product depth may be narrower than mega-banks, and services like investment management are delivered through third-party partnerships rather than proprietary teams. Competitive CD rates (3.85% APY for 9-month terms) are available but require minimum deposits ($2,500-$5,000) and represent fixed-term commitments. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificate of Deposit (CD) accounts with terms up to 24 months
School Spirit Debit Card with school donation feature
Investment management and wealth building strategies
pros: Competitive CD rates: 3.85% APY on 9-month CDs, 3.80% APY on 18-month CDs, 3.70% APY on 24-month IRAs
School Spirit Debit Card donates $0.05 per purchase to local schools, generating meaningful community contributions ($11,706.50 reported)
Free financial literacy and credit education sessions offered at multiple branch locations (April 23 events advertised)
Local lending team approach for mortgages with emphasis on personalized service and transparent communication
Comprehensive investment and insurance services including retirement planning, estate planning, and succession planning
Business banking with remote deposit and dedicated login portal for business customers
Multiple service channels: online banking, mobile access, in-person branches, and phone support
cons: Limited geographic footprint: appears to operate only in Minnesota (Augusta, Polson, Rush City branches mentioned), restricting accessibility for out-of-state customers
Investment services provided through third-party partnership (Cetera Investment Services) rather than proprietary team, which may limit customization
CD minimum deposits ($2,500-$5,000) may be barriers for smaller savers compared to some online banks
No mention of checking account bonus offers, fee structures, or overdraft policies on homepage—important comparison factors are unclear
Regional bank scale means potentially fewer ATMs and branch locations than national competitors, though ATM network details not fully specified
best_for: Minnesota residents seeking personalized mortgage lending with local relationship-based service
Families who want to support local schools through everyday debit card purchases
Business owners in covered markets needing comprehensive banking plus succession and estate planning support
Savers with $2,500+ seeking competitive CD rates for 9-24 month terms
diagnosis: Unity Bank is best for Minnesota residents seeking personal relationships with their banker, particularly those buying homes or managing businesses locally. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the bank operates only in Minnesota, making it inaccessible for out-of-state customers, and its smaller scale means fewer ATM/branch locations than national competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
